comment: tactical-violent bot band for --sim 5000 --policy tactical (batch 10). This bot routes AROUND fights (tactical_routing_map stamps live monsters as walls), heals at <=2/3 HP, and engages only when cornered by an unescapable adjacent threat (a pure route-around bot wins 0/5000 — see the batch-10 status log and docs/design/2026-07-22-mercy-economy-arc.md). It is a COMPETENT-player proxy; the greedy bot (a too-dumb reference player that only fights what blocks its beeline) wins just 17%, which is why the whole difficulty instrument was re-anchored. Measured on the CURRENT (pre-combat-hardening, pre-light-as-grace) game: wins 4163/5000 (83.3%), deaths_combat 497, deaths_dark 340, stuck 0, kills_total 125898. This band RECORDS the current baseline; the design-canon target is ~50% AFTER batch 11's combat hardening + batch 12's light-as-grace, at which point this band is re-anchored under that sign-off. The 2/3 heal threshold, the monster-avoidant routing, and the cornered-engage rule are the tunable knobs. Re-baselining is a balance change: human sign-off required (batches 11-12 grant it). Band calibrated for 5000 seeds; brackets center on the measured values with regression headroom (win_pct 83 -> [75,90]; deaths_dark 340 -> [200,500], intentionally wide because route-around wandering makes dark death a high-variance signal).
